people.name: Carlos Griffith
people.title: President
people.persona: Carlos Griffith runs the firm the way he advises clients to run their plants: on discipline, plain numbers, and a paper trail. He drafts in longhand before anything is typed, keeps the annual workbook close, and reads every proposal that leaves the office. His prose is courteous and unadorned, heavy on complete sentences and light on adjectives, and he signs off with a formal closing even in short memos. He addresses clients by company name and expects the same restraint from everyone who writes under the firm's letterhead.

people.name: Kimberly Price
people.title: Senior Consultant
people.persona: Kimberly Price is the firm's steadiest hand on client engagements, the consultant sent when a manufacturer's operations problem is tangled and the client is anxious. She writes in a measured, methodical voice, laying out findings before recommendations and never promising more than the fieldwork supports. Her steering-committee decks are spare bullet slides with no ornament, and her memos close politely but get to the point in the first paragraph.

people.name: Andrea Diaz
people.title: Consultant
people.persona: Andrea Diaz does much of the on-site legwork, walking distribution floors with a clipboard and turning her notes into clean, workmanlike drafts for review. Her writing is earnest and a touch formal, as befits someone still building her standing with clients, and she is careful to refer to colleagues by full name in anything a client might see. She asks good questions in memos and flags what she does not yet know rather than papering over it.

people.name: Sarah Miller
people.title: Research Associate
people.persona: Sarah Miller is the firm's digger, the one who works the trade directories, phone interviews, and library periodicals until the background section holds up. Her research memos are dense but orderly, with sources noted and hedged language wherever the figures are secondhand. In tone she is precise and self-effacing, letting the material speak and leaving the recommendations to the consultants.

people.name: Jack Reyes
people.title: Office Administrator
people.persona: Jack Reyes keeps the office running: he logs the incoming faxes, scans countersigned engagement letters into the share, and maintains the filing conventions everyone else relies on. His written voice is brisk and administrative, favoring short cover notes and dated routing slips over long memos. He is unfailingly courteous on the phone and in correspondence, and he quietly corrects formatting before anything is printed for the file.

timeline.summary: Firm moved into its current office.
